checks.finding: Every design decision carries an explicit rationale. Technical Summary and Components justify reusing parseEvidenceBundle/parseVerificationContract/path-guard/envelope by citing the established sdd-forge-mcp base (Issue #60). Deployment/CI Plan justifies node:crypto ('追加依存なし。crypto は node 標準') and esbuild dist-commit ('ADR-0003 を踏襲'). Host-deferred git ancestry is justified via ADR-0008 (no-exec boundary); verbatim host formulas via ADR-0009 (agreement). No inline 'We will use X' without a because/踏襲/ADR rationale.

The single entry OQ-001 (design.md lines 246-253) is fully specified: Owner '実装タスク担当' (a role, not TBD), 'Blocks Implementation: no (default で解消済み)', and a concrete Resolution Path 'ADR-0008 承認 → API/Contract Plan gitCommit 節に反映済み'. Consistent with requirements.md OQ-001. No blocking open question lacks a resolution path.

Both assumptions (design.md lines 237-242) are grounded with a cited basis (ASSUMPTIONS-VALID criterion 2). Assumption 1 defers to requirements.md Assumptions, which name generate/check-evidence-bundle.sh as canonical — quoted verbatim in the design's 'API/Contract Plan 正準式' section. Assumption 2 (parseEvidenceBundle echoes signature as-is and reads no key) cites the evidence.ts module doc and path-guard denylist as 確認済み. This brownfield extension grounds each non-trivial claim in cited, in-repo artifacts, so there is no ungrounded empirical assumption requiring investigation.md.

design.md has a '## Constraint Compliance' section (lines 226-235) that addresses every requirements constraint: REQ-008 no-key/no-sig-verify, 'without external tools / no new exec', read-only, REQ-009 host-script agreement, REQ-010 determinism, REQ-012 additive contract backward-compat. Security boundaries B1-B3 (requirements Security Boundaries / security-spec.md) are each addressed. No constraint is contradicted or left without a compliance statement.

No performance requirements (response time, throughput, concurrency, latency SLA / numeric threshold) found in requirements.md. The only numeric bound is the 2 MiB path-guard read limit, which is a DoS/resource guard and is addressed in design (per-artifact 'too-large' status, Deployment/CI Plan and Test Strategy error-path tests).

'## Deployment / CI Plan' (lines 215-224) names the deployment target (monorepo-nested package mcp/sdd-forge-mcp), distribution (esbuild single bundle + dist-commit + dist-parity CI, ADR-0003), runtime requirement (Node >= 20, no new deps), CI changes (rides existing .github/workflows/test.yml sdd-forge-mcp job; no new job), and rollback (single PR revert restores dist). Incremental feature-flag rollout and DB migration ordering are justifiably N/A: additive single read-only tool, Data Plan declares no schema changes.

Data Plan (lines 78-89) declares 'Data Entities: なし', 'Existing Data Affected: なし(書込みなし)', and 'Migration Strategy: 不要(read-only ツール追加)'. No schema/table/column changes; contract change is additive (v1 maintained). MIGRATION-PLANNED PASS per the 'No data changes' condition.

No external system, third-party service, or cross-service runtime API call exists. The Architecture and Deployment sections establish the tool is fully local: no network, no git/python subprocess, no fs write, all reads via path-guard chokepoint. Host scripts (generate/check-evidence-bundle.sh) are referenced only as the canonical-formula parity source (ADR-0009), not invoked at runtime, so no runtime integration contract/failure-behaviour section is required.

No scope creep: the design adds exactly one tool (evidence_deep_verify) plus the additive evidenceDeepVerifyData contract shape, and every Component maps to REQ-001..REQ-013 via the Cross-Layer Dependencies table. Conversely all requirements.md Goals (read-only deep-verify, host-formula agreement, no-key boundary) and User Stories (verdict for Done gating, tamper surfacing, no-key guarantee) are covered by design sections and the AC-001..AC-019 mapping. No component/endpoint lacks a corresponding requirement, and no required capability is omitted.

The applicable high-risk surface (security/authorization: signature-key non-read and no-signature-verify boundary B3, plus integration failure behaviour for missing/oversize artifacts) has concrete named validation paths in Test Strategy and security-spec Security Tests: no-key/canary test TEST-011 (tests/no-secrets/), static read-only/no-exec check TEST-014 (tests/readonly/), host-parity golden TEST-012 (tests/golden/), and error-path tests TEST-004/005 (tests/error-paths/). These are named, inspectable planned evidence artifacts, not live-execution requirements.
